title: rubus canescens var. glabratus
intro: Rubus canescens var. glabratus is a variant of the Rubus canescens plant, also known as the gray thimbleberry, a type of shrub in the rose family native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus canescens var. glabratus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. Rubus canescens var. glabratus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
